app.title: Planetary Imaging Pipeline
settings.title: Global Settings
settings.desc: Configure observation target, filters, and camera mode.
step01.title: Step 1 — PIPP Preprocessing
step01.desc: Reject poor-quality frames and crop to planet center from SER files.
step02.title: Step 2 — AutoStakkert 4 (External Tool)
step02.desc: Run AS!4 separately and proceed to Step 3 when done.
step02.instructions:
1. Launch AutoStakkert 4.
2. Load your SER files and run stacking.
3. AS!4 will save TIF files to an output folder (e.g. capture_folder/AS_P25/).
4. When done, click 'Continue' below to proceed to Step 3.
5. In Step 3, point the input folder to where AS!4 saved the TIF files.
step02.hint2: You can skip Step 1 (PIPP) and go straight to Step 3 — just set the AS!4 TIF folder there.
step03.title: Step 3 — Wavelet Preview
step03.desc: Apply wavelet sharpening to each AS!4 TIF and generate preview PNGs.
step03.output_dir.placeholder: Parent folder where all step results are saved
step04.title: Step 4 — Quality Assessment
step04.desc: Score each frame and select de-rotation windows.
step05.title: Step 5 — De-rotation Stacking
step05.desc: Correct for planetary rotation and stack aligned frames.
step06.title: Step 6 — Wavelet Master
step06.desc: Apply wavelet sharpening to the stacked master images.
step07.title: Step 7 — RGB Composite (Master)
step07.desc: Combine filter masters into color composite images.
step08.title: Step 8 — Time-series Composite
step08.desc: Composite each time-slot filter set to show planetary changes over time.
step09.title: Step 9 — Animated GIF
step09.desc: Create an animated GIF from the time-series composites.
step10.title: Step 10 — Summary Grid
step10.desc: Arrange master composites into a summary contact sheet PNG.
color_mode.placeholder:
Color camera mode is coming in a future version.
Please use the monochrome camera workflow for now.
msg.restart_required: Language change will apply after restart.
